channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok starts testing Footnotes, a new feature that looks a lot like X’s Community Notes
item.description: TikTok is launching its own version of community notes on the platform, called “Footnotes.” The crowd-sourced approach to moderation, where users add additional context to posts, has become increasingly popular on social media networks after X (then called Twitter) first launched it in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Gleam 1.10 improves compiler, JavaScript codegen
item.description: Gleam 1.10, a new release of the type-safe programming language for the Erlang virtual machine and JavaScript runtimes, is now available. The update features compiler improvements centering on holding more information and improving exhaustive analysis.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scammers Cost Consumers Estimated $470 Million in 2024: FTC Report
item.description: According to a new report from the Federal Trade Commission, scammers cost consumers an estimated hundreds of millions of dollars in scams that started off with a text message.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s China tariffs spark viral TikTok work-arounds
item.description: President Trump’s trade wars have officially landed on TikTok. U.S. TikTok users’ For You Pages are being flooded with videos from Chinese manufacturers urging Americans to bypass sweeping new tariffs by purchasing goods directly from China.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU equips US-bound staff with burner phones
item.description: The European Commission has issued so-called burner phones and simple laptops to certain staffers traveling to the US to reduce the risk of espionage, according to The Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI brings order to observability disorder
item.description: Digital tools are the lifeblood of today’s enterprises, but the complexity of hybrid cloud architectures, involving thousands of containers, microservices and applications, frustrates operational leaders trying to optimize business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Deezer is rolling out its answer to Wrapped and Replay, plus three new features you won't find on Spotify or Apple Music
item.description: Deezer's launching four new features between now and the end of May – and top of the pile has to be platform-agnostic music sharing
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Android Auto-Reboot Feature Rolling Out, Restarting Devices After Three Days of Inactivity
item.description: The latest Google Play services update includes a feature that will restart your phone or tablet automatically after three days of inactivity, providing an added layer of security against unauthorized access.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will reportedly begin soon in Canada, and I'm hoping that the US follows suit
item.description: A major leaker claims that Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will start soon in Canada but the US date is still up in the air.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: AI system restores speech for paralyzed patients using own voice
item.description: Developed at two California universities, the innovative technology combines brain-computer interfaces with advanced AI to decode neural activity into audible speech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft at 50: The 7 biggest game-changers through five decades
item.description: When Microsoft was founded 50 years ago this month, it wasn’t clear the company would last 50 weeks, much less 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 languages you can deploy to WebAssembly right now
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, gives developers a way to create programs that run at near-native speed in the browser or anywhere else you can deploy the WebAssembly runtime. But you generally don’t write programs in Wasm directly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Los CIO están gastando de más en la nube, pero siguen pensando que vale la pena
item.description: Los importantes aumentos imprevistos en el gasto en la nube, impulsados en gran medida por un mayor uso, no están llevando a los CIO a replantearse las estrategias de TI más allá de los cambios tácticos en la forma en que intentan controlar los costes en la nube.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legacy broadcasters account for just more than 10 percent of TV viewership in March
item.description: Legacy media companies amassed only 10 percent of total television viewership during the month of March, according to new figures from Nielsen Media Research. Nielsen's monthly TV viewership report noted Disney, Paramount, Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Can't find your Android phone? Here's what to do to track it down
item.description: There are multiple ways to find your missing Android phone after it goes missing. One method involves using a smartwatch to ping your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How thieves use new credit card numbers before you receive them
item.description: The CyberGuy provides tips to protect yourself from criminals who use various methods to make unauthorized transactions using your account information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cutting-edge drone tech maps land and water with laser accuracy
item.description: New drone technology maps land and water with stunning accuracy, giving researchers and conservationists a new way to understand our planet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
